title: Quiz: Attack Vectors in Quantum Networks

questions.question: How does a detector blinding attack work?

options.text: It blocks all light from reaching the detector
Bright light forces detector into linear mode, allowing Eve to control clicks

questions.explanation: Detector blinding uses bright continuous light to saturate single-photon detectors, forcing them from Geiger mode into linear mode. Eve then controls when detectors click using trigger pulses.

questions.question: What is a Trojan horse attack in QKD?

options.text: A virus that infects the QKD software
Injecting probe light to read internal device settings
Replacing the QKD device with a fake

questions.explanation: A Trojan horse attack involves Eve injecting bright light into Alice or Bob's device. The light reflects off internal components (modulators, phase settings), revealing information about basis choices or key bits.

questions.question: How does the photon number splitting (PNS) attack exploit weak coherent sources?

options.text: By splitting multi-photon pulses and storing one photon

questions.explanation: Weak coherent sources sometimes emit multiple photons. Eve can split off one photon, store it in a quantum memory, then measure it after basis reconciliation reveals Alice's basis choice.

questions.question: What type of information can timing side-channels leak?

options.text: The physical location of the device
Basis choices or key bits based on operation duration

questions.explanation: If different operations (like modulating different bases) take different amounts of time, an attacker can measure timing to infer which operation was performed, potentially revealing basis choices or key bits.

questions.question: What is the main vulnerability of trusted relay networks?

options.text: They are slower than direct links
A compromised relay node can access all relayed keys

questions.explanation: In trusted relay networks, intermediate nodes must briefly possess the key material to perform XOR operations. A compromised relay node can read all keys passing through it.

questions.question: What is the primary defense against man-in-the-middle attacks on QKD?
questions.explanation: Without authentication, Eve can establish separate QKD sessions with Alice and Bob, completely compromising security. Authentication of the classical channel prevents impersonation.

questions.question: Measurement-device-independent QKD eliminates all side-channel attacks.
questions.explanation: False. MDI-QKD eliminates detector side-channel attacks but not source-side attacks. The transmitter can still have side-channels (timing, power, Trojan horse at source). Full protection requires device-independent QKD.
